title: Buttermilk (Chaas)
description: Cool, spiced Indian buttermilk made from thick peanut or plant curd—dairy-like tang without milk. Perfect with spicy meals or as a light drink.
explanation: Classic chaas is diluted curd whisked with salt, cumin, and herbs. Using well-set homemade cashew-almond curd keeps the body and tang closer to dairy buttermilk than thin soy milk alone.
This is a drink and a meal-cooler, not a protein shake. Pair with dal-rice or a heavy lunch when you miss the glass of chaas after a thali.
ingredients: 1 cup Thick Homemade Curd (cashew-almond; or unsweetened plant yogurt with body)
1/4 tsp Black Salt (kala namak) or to taste
instructions: Whisk the curd until completely smooth with no lumps—use a whisk, mathani, or a few short blender pulses.
Add cold water gradually while whisking until you reach a pourable buttermilk consistency (slightly thinner than a lassi, not watery like plain water).
Stir in roasted cumin, black salt, and regular salt. Taste and adjust tang with a squeeze of lemon only if the curd was mild.
Add green chilli, ginger, coriander, and mint if using. Chill 15 minutes or pour over ice.
Serve immediately so herbs stay bright. Stir before each glass if it separates slightly.
stepNotes: Start with thick curd; thin liquid yogurt makes flat, sour water instead of chaas.
Black salt adds the familiar edge many people associate with dairy chaas—use lightly.
Cashew-almond homemade curd gives the cleanest chaas. Soy curd works if unsweetened and well whisked; peanut curd will taste of peanut.
Do not boil plant curd for this drink—heat can split it. Keep everything cold.
Make the base without chilli for kids; offer chilli as a side muddle.

variationSections.title: Regional styles
items: Gujarati-style: a pinch of sugar with cumin and ginger.
Punjabi mattha: more black salt, more mint, coarser whisk.
South Indian moru: curry leaves and mustard tempered in a spoon of oil, cooled, then stirred in.

variationSections.title: Thickness and use
items: Thicker (less water) as a side raita-drink with rice.
Thinner over ice for summer hydration.
Blend with cucumber for a cooling cucumber chaas.
